constraints.text: Precautions typical of field air sampling projects are required.
Tripping, falling, electrical, and weather safety considerations must all be included in plans to collect source canister samples.
The user is encouraged to establish safety procedures before using this method.
Exercise extreme care in working with high-pressure gas cylinders.
Exposure to suspected carcinogens or other compounds with serious health risks should be avoided in all circumstances.
Refer to the safety data sheet (SDS) of each specific compound for safe handling instructions.

steps.text: Clean glassware and sampling apparatus thoroughly before use.
Soak glassware (including impingers, stems, and caps) in hot soapy water at 50 °C or higher.
Rinse glassware three times with hot tap water.
Rinse glassware three times with DI water.
Rinse glassware three times with methanol.
Bake glassware in a vacuum oven at 65 – 100 °C with greater than 67 kPa vacuum for a minimum of 2 hours.
Rinse impingers with DI water and refill the impingers between runs.
Soak coated stainless-steel fittings, orifices, and transfer line in methanol for at least five minutes.
Decant and repeat soaking in methanol three times.
Soak coated stainless-steel fittings, orifices, and transfer line in water for at least five minutes.
Decant and repeat soaking in water three times.
Close the canister valve.
Record the stop time.
Perform a post-test leak check.
Disconnect the canister from the train.
Confirm the final canister vacuum.
Attach the vacuum gauge to the canister and open the canister valve.
Record the final pressure.
Compare the final pressure and associated sample volume to the theoretical volume sampled.
Close the valve, remove the gauge, and replace the canister inlet cap.
Collect the necessary data/minimal information for each sample.

Generate an analytical sequence and sample batch that at a minimum includes the following samples: One LB at the beginning of the sequence, a daily CCV after the LB, and field samples, including one duplicate analysis of a field sample.
Run the sample sequence.
At the conclusion of data acquisition, use the same software settings established during the calibration procedure to qualitatively identify analyte peaks in the predetermined retention time windows.
Confirm the identity of each analyte by comparing its retention time with that of the corresponding target analyte peak in an ICAL standard or CCV.
Confirm that the ions are consistent with the continuing calibration results.

After the entire sequence has been analyzed on the instrument, review the analytical results (chromatograms, peak integrations, and instrument reports) as described in Section 9 for completeness and performance requirements prior to data reporting.
Reanalyze samples that failed performance criteria.
Dilute samples as necessary to measure target analytes within the calibration range.
Record and report deviations to performance criteria and actions to report compliant data.
Calculate the concentration (ppbv) of each target compound in the sample canister using Eq. 13 of this method.

Source gas volume calculations. Carry out calculations for stack gas velocity, volumetric flow rate, sampling volume, moisture, proportional sampling rate using equations 15 through 19 in Section 12 of this method.

Calculate and report wet and dry gas concentrations found in source/vent samples correcting for sample dilutions using Eq. 16 or Eq.17 in units of ppbv and µg/dscm using Eq. 18 in Section 12 of this method.
Record and report sample specific MDLs inclusive of sample-specific dilutions, final volumes, aliquots, etc.
Identify unknown peaks.
Select ions based on ion abundance, uniqueness, and lack of interference with other target compounds and internal standards.
Perform a leak check before and after each test run.
Inspect/tighten train components/connections and retry leak check.
Void any test run that doesn’t pass post leak-check.
Flag sample data for possible rejection if the holding time exceeds 30 days from sample collection.
Ensure that the measured target compound mass in each fraction is reported and used to assess the impact on results.
Check the actual volume collected after each test run for within 20% RPD of theoretical volume.
If the actual volume collected is >20% RPD, check for leaks and void the sample.
If the actual volume is >20% RPD, check the critical orifice for plugging and replace or clean as needed.
Ensure sufficient volume is collected to achieve the project specific detection limits and flag the sample on the COC.
Measure the pressure of each canister upon receipt and compare the volume collected as received to the field post-test volume.
Flag the sample for potential exclusion from the data set if the volume as received is >5% RPD.
Calibrate dynamic gas dilution systems annually against NIST traceable primary flow standards with uncertainties <=0.25%.
Calibrate static gas dilution systems annually ensuring pressure transducer(s) have an accuracy of ± 0.1 % full scale or 0.13 kPa, whichever is smaller.
Perform ICAL analysis of a minimum of five calibration levels before sample analysis, annually or after a failing CCV, or when changes/maintenance to the instrument affect calibration response.
Check for leaks, perform maintenance, and reanalyze.
Prepare fresh standards and reanalyze ICAL.
Flag any target analytes that are manually integrated in the calibration curve.
Analyze laboratory blank canister filled with humidified (50% RH) clean diluent gas after high ICAL standard and at the beginning of each analytical sequence.
Check instrument for contamination and reanalyze.
Prepare a fresh laboratory blank and reanalyze.
Analyze a canister of all target VFC prepared at the MDL after each MDL study (annually or after significant instrument maintenance/repair).
Verify that the primary quantitation ion is detected at 3 times signal to noise and the retention time is within ±5 seconds of the initial calibration standard.
Reprepare the MDL Confirmation standard.
Reanalyze the MDL study and repeat confirmation.
Check for instrument leaks and reanalyze.
Prepare fresh standard and reanalyze.
Check for leaks in the chromatographic system, repair and reanalyze.
Flag any sample data that does not meet IS retention time criteria.

preconditions.text: The lowest calibration concentration must be ≥3x MDL.
Must be within ±30% of each target compound spike concentration.
Retention time must be within +/-5 seconds of the most recent calibration.

steps.text: Check for leaks in the analytical system, perform maintenance and reanalyze.
Retune and recalibrate instrument and reanalyze.
Flag any sample results with failing IS responses.
Reanalyze all samples after the last passing CCV.
Check instrument for leaks and reanalyze.
Re-prep and reanalyze CCV.
Perform instrument maintenance and reanalyze.
Clean the MS source and rerun the ICAL.
Flag target analytes that do not meet ending CCV criteria.
Flag the field sample and target analytes that did not meet acceptance criteria.
Analyze one or more cleaned canister as an LB from a given batch of clean canisters.
Analyze other cans in batch as LBs.
Check canister cleaning system for contamination.
Reclean failed canisters.
Optimize the analytical system to reduce/eliminate bias, recalibrate, and reanalyze.
Flag any sample results generated using a system that exceeds the ±30 percent bias threshold.
